name: NIPS 2017 Adversarial Attacks 1st Place -- Momentum Iterative FGSM

stages.name: Ensemble Prediction Label Inference
stages.description: Infers true label from ensemble consensus on first iteration only, then freezes for subsequent iterations. Avoids label leakage from competition infrastructure.
stages.dejargonized_description: Ask all models what class the image is, take the majority vote as the true label, and keep that label fixed for the entire attack.

inputs.name: predictions_list
inputs.constraints: Prediction logits from each model in ensemble.

inputs.name: previous_labels
inputs.constraints: Labels from prior iteration (ignored on iteration 0).

outputs.name: attack_labels
outputs.constraints: One-hot encoded labels for loss computation.

preconditions: At least one model prediction available.

stages.name: Adaptive Epsilon Attack Strategy
stages.description: Selects model count, iteration count, and step size based on perturbation budget. eps >= 8: 5 models, 20 iters, alpha=eps/12. eps < 8: 2 models, 40 iters, alpha=eps/28. With tight budget, precision matters more; with large budget, model diversity matters more.
stages.dejargonized_description: Choose how many models to use and how many steps to take based on how much room you have to change the image -- tight budgets need more precise, smaller steps with fewer models.
guarantees: step_size * num_iterations approximately covers epsilon budget.

stages.name: Ensemble Logit Fusion with Asymmetric Weights
stages.description: Weighted average of logits from multiple models. Adversarially-trained models (adv_inception_v3) get weight 0.25 while standard models get 1.0, preventing perturbations from overfitting to the adversarial training distribution.
stages.dejargonized_description: Average the raw predictions from all models, but give adversarially-hardened models less influence so the attack generalizes to new models.

inputs.name: weights
inputs.constraints: Model name to weight mapping. Adversarially-trained models should be downweighted.

preconditions: All logits have same shape [B, C].
guarantees: Output shape matches input logit shape.

stages.name: Auxiliary Logit Loss Fusion
stages.description: Backpropagates through both main and auxiliary classification heads with weight 0.4 on auxiliary. Produces richer gradients capturing feature sensitivity at multiple network depths. Correctly excludes models without auxiliary heads (e.g., ResNet).
stages.dejargonized_description: Compute the attack loss using both the final prediction and an intermediate prediction from each model, so the attack signal captures what the network cares about at multiple layers.

inputs.name: aux_logits
inputs.constraints: Auxiliary head logits (None for models without aux head).

preconditions: Main logits always available. Aux logits may be None.
guarantees: Loss is differentiable w.r.t. input images.

stages.name: Momentum Iterative Gradient Accumulation (MI-FGSM)
stages.description: L1-normalizes current gradient, accumulates with momentum mu=1.0. The paper's headline contribution. Full momentum means accumulated gradient has equal weight to new gradient. L1-normalization prevents any single iteration's gradient magnitude from dominating. Dramatically improves transferability to unseen models.
stages.dejargonized_description: Keep a running average of past attack directions, normalizing each new direction before adding it in, so the attack follows a smoother path that works on models it has never seen.

inputs.name: previous_accumulated
inputs.constraints: Accumulated gradient from prior iterations (zeros on first).

preconditions: Gradient has been computed from ensemble loss.
guarantees: Output magnitude is controlled by L1 normalization.

stages.name: Std-Normalized Momentum Gradient (Targeted Variant)
stages.description: Std-normalization applied TWICE: once to raw gradient before accumulation, once to accumulated result. Preserves relative spatial structure better than L1-mean, important for targeted attacks where perturbation must push toward a specific class boundary.
stages.dejargonized_description: A variant of the momentum accumulation that normalizes by standard deviation instead of average, applied twice to keep the attack signal stable when pushing toward a specific target class.
preconditions: Gradient computed from targeted loss (negated cross-entropy).
guarantees: Output has controlled magnitude via std normalization.

stages.name: Rounded Clipped Perturbation Step
stages.description: Uses round(noise) clipped to [-2, 2] instead of sign(noise). Produces integer values {-2,-1,0,1,2}. Multi-level quantized step allows larger per-pixel moves where gradient is confident, zero-steps where near-zero. Exploits perturbation budget more efficiently for targeted attacks.
stages.dejargonized_description: Instead of nudging every pixel by the same amount, nudge confident pixels more and uncertain pixels less, using a 5-level scale instead of just plus/minus one.
guarantees: Each pixel delta is an integer multiple of alpha, bounded by clip_range.

edges.provenance: Ensemble-inferred labels used as attack target for loss computation.
Strategy determines how many models participate in ensemble.
Asymmetrically-weighted ensemble logits fed to loss.
Loss differentiated w.r.t. input to produce gradient (tf.gradients).
Loss differentiated w.r.t. input for targeted variant.
Std-normalized accumulated gradient feeds into multi-level quantized step.

bindings.atom_code_html:
@register_atom(witness_ensemble_prediction_label_inference)
@icontract.require(
    lambda predictions: len(predictions) >= 1,
    "need at least one prediction array",
)
@icontract.require(
    lambda iteration, previous_labels: iteration == 0 or previous_labels is not None,
    "previous_labels must be provided when iteration > 0",
)
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda predictions, result: result.shape == (predictions[0].shape[0],),
    "output must have shape (B,)",
)
def ensemble_prediction_label_inference(
    predictions: list[NDArray[np.float64]],
    iteration: int = 0,
    previous_labels: NDArray[np.int64] | None = None,

@register_atom(witness_adaptive_epsilon_strategy)
@icontract.require(lambda max_epsilon: max_epsilon > 0.0, "max_epsilon must be positive")
@icontract.require(lambda threshold: threshold > 0.0, "threshold must be positive")
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda result: all(k in result for k in ("n_models", "n_iterations", "step_size")),
    "result must contain n_models, n_iterations, and step_size keys",
)
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda result: result["n_models"] >= 1 and result["n_iterations"] >= 1 and result["step_size"] > 0.0,
    "all configuration values must be positive",
)
def adaptive_epsilon_strategy(
    max_epsilon: float,
    threshold: float = 8.0,
bindings.swap_rationale: Fixed configuration (5 models, 20 iterations) works for most budgets. The adaptive strategy optimizes for edge cases.

bindings.atom_code_html:
@register_atom(witness_ensemble_logit_fusion)
@icontract.require(
    lambda logits, weights: len(logits) == len(weights),
    "number of logit arrays must match number of weights",
)
@icontract.require(lambda logits: len(logits) >= 1, "need at least one model")
@icontract.require(
    lambda weights: all(w >= 0.0 for w in weights),
    "all weights must be non-negative",
)
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda logits, result: result.shape == logits[0].shape,
    "fused output shape must match individual logit shape",
)
def ensemble_logit_fusion(
    logits: list[NDArray[np.float64]],
    weights: NDArray[np.float64],

@register_atom(witness_auxiliary_logit_loss_fusion)
@icontract.require(
    lambda main_logits, labels_onehot: main_logits.shape == labels_onehot.shape,
    "main_logits and labels_onehot must have the same shape",
)
@icontract.require(
    lambda main_logits, aux_logits: aux_logits is None or main_logits.shape == aux_logits.shape,
    "aux_logits must have the same shape as main_logits if provided",
)
@icontract.require(lambda aux_weight: aux_weight >= 0.0, "aux_weight must be non-negative")
@icontract.require(
    lambda labels_onehot: np.allclose(labels_onehot.sum(axis=1), 1.0, atol=0.01),
    "labels_onehot rows must sum to approximately 1",
)
@icontract.ensure(lambda result: result >= 0.0 and bool(np.isfinite(result)), "loss must be finite and non-negative")
def auxiliary_logit_loss_fusion(
    main_logits: NDArray[np.float64],
    labels_onehot: NDArray[np.float64],
    aux_logits: NDArray[np.float64] | None = None,
    aux_weight: float = 0.4,
bindings.swap_rationale: Standard cross-entropy loss works without auxiliary heads. The auxiliary loss adds ~2% transfer rate improvement on Inception models.

bindings.atom_code_html:
@register_atom(witness_momentum_gradient_accumulation)
@icontract.require(
    lambda gradient, previous_accumulated: gradient.shape == previous_accumulated.shape,
    "gradient and previous_accumulated must have the same shape",
)
@icontract.require(lambda momentum: momentum >= 0.0, "momentum must be non-negative")
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda gradient, result: result.shape == gradient.shape,
    "output must preserve the input shape",
)
def momentum_gradient_accumulation(
    gradient: NDArray[np.float64],
    previous_accumulated: NDArray[np.float64],
    momentum: float = 1.0,

@register_atom(witness_std_normalized_momentum_gradient)
@icontract.require(
    lambda gradient, previous_accumulated: gradient.shape == previous_accumulated.shape,
    "gradient and previous_accumulated must have the same shape",
)
@icontract.require(lambda momentum: momentum >= 0.0, "momentum must be non-negative")
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda gradient, result: result.shape == gradient.shape,
    "output must preserve the input shape",
)
def std_normalized_momentum_gradient(
    gradient: NDArray[np.float64],
    previous_accumulated: NDArray[np.float64],
    momentum: float = 1.0,

@register_atom(witness_rounded_clipped_perturbation_step)
@icontract.require(lambda clip_range: clip_range >= 1, "clip_range must be at least 1")
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda noise, result: result.shape == noise.shape,
    "output must preserve the input shape",
)
@icontract.ensure(
    lambda clip_range, result: np.all(result >= -clip_range) and np.all(result <= clip_range),
    "all values must be within [-clip_range, clip_range]",
)
def rounded_clipped_perturbation_step(
    noise: NDArray[np.float64],
    clip_range: int = 2,
bindings.swap_rationale: Standard sign-based stepping (FGSM-style) works for non-targeted attacks. The rounded multi-level step is mainly beneficial for targeted attacks.
